title: Idempotency key handling
implementation_summary: Added the control-plane Claude review Idempotency key handling slice for RP00.P02.M04.S10.

The S10 policy, result-field registry, prechecker module, service function, validator, synthetic fixture, contract definition, README, and RP00 validator bind a frozen metadata-only idempotency receipt after RP00.P02.M04.S09.

The precheck consumes the S09 State transition enforcement result and requires the S01 service entrypoint contract, S02 normalized request, S03 tenant boundary precheck, S04 Matter trace precheck, S05 Permission precheck, S06 Audit hint precheck, S07 Primary happy path result, S08 Secondary workflow path result, S09 State transition enforcement result, RP00.P02.M03.S20 Hermes Validation denied-path evidence refs, and the permission/audit baseline RP00.P00.M05.S01 to remain intact.

The allowed path emits an allow_metadata_only idempotency receipt only when the carried S09 state transition decision is allow_metadata_only, the S09 result is marked applied, the entrypoint is claude_review, the model is claude-opus-4-8, effort is max, read-only review metadata remains present, an audit event ref is present, and the idempotency key is a safe synthetic idem.synthetic.* key.

Missing S09 results, wrong upstream markers, denied state transitions, non-Claude-review entrypoints, wrong model or effort, non-read-only review metadata, missing receipts, missing audit refs, invalid idempotency keys, idempotency bypass/store/record claims, lock acquisition claims, service execution claims, runtime route claims, product write claims, runtime Claude/review queue claims, audit write claims, runtime permission/AuthZ/permission-engine claims, source-completion claims, and unknown truthy idempotency claims fail closed.

The S10 result hard-codes no idempotency record write, no idempotency store write, no lock acquisition, no audit ledger append, no audit event write, no runtime Claude invocation, no review queue write, no review assignment write, no review notification, no service logic execution, no runtime route creation, no runtime permission evaluation, no AuthZ evaluator call, no permission engine execution, no product-state write, no database write, and no storage write.

The dedicated service tests prove policy binding, metadata-only idempotency receipt emission, explicit synthetic key acceptance, result drift rejection, unsafe-claim and wrong-source failures, unsafe-key rejection, prechecker no-write boundaries, S11 handoff, and synthetic fixture parity.

The RP00 validator now enforces S10 contract definition, policy module, service module, validators, fixture metadata, weighted-ledger presence, and construction inspection flag control_plane_claude_review_idempotency_key_handling_verified.

The boundary is metadata-only and does not persist idempotency records, write idempotency stores, acquire locks, append audit ledgers, write audit events, invoke Claude at runtime, mutate review queues, replace human approval, write storage, write databases, or persist product state.
RP00.P02.M04 remains open after S10 and hands off to RP00.P02.M04.S11 Lock acquisition rule; RP00.P02 and RP00 remain open.
